input: Evaluate meta-conductor against the Five Criteria
expectedDiagnosis: Five Criteria item-by-item verification with evidence table for meta-conductor

expectedBehavior.evaluation: Fill evidence table for each of the 5 criteria: Independent, Small Enough, Clear Boundaries, Replaceable, Reusable
expectedBehavior.output: Evidence table with specific evidence and Pass/Fail per criterion

diagnosticQuestions: Does meta-conductor have clear boundaries? (Own X, Do Not Touch Y)
Can meta-conductor be replaced without collapsing the system?
When other metas take the field, will there be Cross-contamination with meta-conductor?
Can meta-conductor combine with other metas?
recommendation: If all 5 Pass → no action needed; if any Fail → identify specific remediation per failed criterion

passFailCriteria.PASS: All 5 criteria evaluated with specific evidence from the agent definition file; Four Questions also applied as runtime judgment
passFailCriteria.FAIL: Criteria evaluated with generic statements lacking specific evidence; Four Questions not applied

input: 一个 agent 什么都能干：理解需求、找文件、设计方案、写代码、验证、解释
expectedDiagnosis: Stew-All / Omnipotent Executor Meta Anti-Pattern detected

expectedBehavior.detection: Identify that the agent spans >2 unrelated domains and performs understanding, file search, design, coding, verification, and explanation all at once
expectedBehavior.evaluation: Check SOUL.md length (>300 lines signals Stew-All); verify if execution happens before thorough understanding
expectedBehavior.output: Flag Omnipotent Executor Meta compression disease; recommend triggering Type B splitting pipeline

diagnosticQuestions: Does this agent have >2 unrelated domains?
Is the SOUL.md >300 lines?
Does execution happen before thorough understanding?
Are decisions made before complete information gathering?
recommendation: Trigger Type B splitting pipeline to decompose into focused agents with single responsibility classes

passFailCriteria.PASS: Stew-All and Omnipotent Executor patterns detected; Type B splitting recommended with specific domain boundaries proposed
passFailCriteria.FAIL: Agent accepted as-is without detecting the anti-pattern; no splitting recommended

input: 这个 agent 管了前端、后端、数据库三块，需要拆分吗？
expectedDiagnosis: Coupling analysis needed — run git co-change data to determine if splitting is warranted

expectedBehavior.detection: Run git log commands to collect co-change data for the three domains (frontend, backend, database)
expectedBehavior.evaluation: Apply coupling criterion: if A changes, does B frequently need to change? High co-change → merge; low co-change → separate
expectedBehavior.output: Data-driven splitting recommendation with co-change frequencies and domain boundary proposal

diagnosticQuestions: Do frontend changes frequently trigger backend changes?
Do backend changes frequently trigger database schema changes?
Is the change frequency for each domain >5% of total changes?
What is the co-change frequency between each pair of domains?
recommendation: Split based on coupling data; present Iron Rule: user overrides data if they insist two domains are different

passFailCriteria.PASS: Git co-change commands executed; coupling criterion applied; Iron Rule presented; data-driven recommendation with specific frequency thresholds
passFailCriteria.FAIL: Splitting decision made without data; git commands not run; Iron Rule not mentioned

input: Runtime diagnosis: is meta-librarian operating correctly within the system?
expectedDiagnosis: Four Questions runtime diagnostic applied to meta-librarian

expectedBehavior.detection: Apply the Meta-Verification Four Questions as runtime judgment rather than design-time Five Criteria
expectedBehavior.evaluation: Answer each of the 4 diagnostic questions with specific evidence from the running system
expectedBehavior.output: Runtime health assessment with specific findings per question and recommended corrective actions

diagnosticQuestions: Does meta-librarian have clear boundaries? (Own X, Do Not Touch Y)
Can meta-librarian be replaced without collapsing?
When other metas take the field, will there be Cross-contamination?
Can meta-librarian combine with other metas?
recommendation: Four Questions are more direct for runtime judgment than Five Criteria; use findings to decide if boundary adjustment is needed

passFailCriteria.PASS: All 4 diagnostic questions answered with runtime evidence; distinction between design-time (Five Criteria) and runtime (Four Questions) acknowledged
passFailCriteria.FAIL: Questions answered generically; no distinction between design-time and runtime checks

input: We have 15 micro-agents — each does one tiny thing and needs 3 other agents to produce any output

expectedBehavior.detection: Identify that individual agents cannot produce output independently; coordination cost exceeds value
expectedBehavior.evaluation: Apply Shattered diagnostic: 'Needs other agents output to produce? Coordination cost > value?'
expectedBehavior.output: Recommend merging co-dependent agents into larger cohesive units until each can produce independently

diagnosticQuestions: Does each agent need other agents' output to produce anything?
Is the coordination cost greater than the value produced?
Can any agent be understood, invoked, and produce output on its own?
Would merging reduce coordination overhead without creating Stew-All?
recommendation: Merge co-dependent agents until each satisfies the Independent criterion; verify merged agents do not become Stew-All

passFailCriteria.PASS: Shattered pattern diagnosed with specific evidence of inter-dependency; merge proposal preserves independence without creating Stew-All
passFailCriteria.FAIL: Pattern not detected; agents left fragmented; or merging creates new Stew-All

input: Our pipeline goes direction → planning → execution. No review step, no meta-review, no evolution.

expectedBehavior.detection: Identify missing review chain: no review, no meta-review (who reviews the reviewers?), no experience codification
expectedBehavior.evaluation: Apply diagnostic: 'Who reviews? Who reviews the reviewers? How is experience codified?'
expectedBehavior.output: Recommend adding review → meta-review → verification → evolution stages to the pipeline

diagnosticQuestions: Who reviews the execution output?
Who reviews the reviewers?
How is experience codified after each run?
Will it still work tomorrow? Can someone else take over?
recommendation: Add Review, Meta-Review, Verification, and Evolution stages; assign specific agents to each review function

passFailCriteria.PASS: Governance-Free Execution pattern diagnosed; missing stages identified; concrete recommendation to add review/meta-review/verification/evolution chain
passFailCriteria.FAIL: Pipeline accepted as sufficient; missing governance stages not detected

input: Agents frequently Cross-contaminate — meta-prism starts designing agents, meta-genesis starts reviewing quality

expectedBehavior.detection: Identify boundary violations: meta-prism operating outside its quality review domain; meta-genesis operating outside its design domain
expectedBehavior.evaluation: Apply Organizational Mirror: mature org has division, escalation, review, fallback — agents must mirror this
expectedBehavior.output: Re-establish clear Own/Do Not Touch boundaries for each agent; verify no Cross-contamination after fix

diagnosticQuestions: When other metas take the field, will there be Cross-contamination?
Are Own and Do Not Touch boundaries explicit for each agent?
Does each agent stay within its declared responsibility class?
Is the Organizational Mirror principle (division/escalation/review/fallback) reflected in agent boundaries?
recommendation: Audit each agent boundary definition; enforce Own/Do Not Touch declarations; use Organizational Mirror to ensure proper division of labor

passFailCriteria.PASS: Cross-contamination identified with specific agent pairs; Organizational Mirror applied to redesign boundaries; Own/Do Not Touch enforced
passFailCriteria.FAIL: Boundary violations not detected; agents allowed to operate outside declared domains

input: We ran the system once and it worked great. Now we treat that single run as the permanent playbook.
expectedDiagnosis: Result-Chasing Without Structure Death Pattern detected

expectedBehavior.detection: Identify that one successful run is being treated as gospel without structural reproducibility
expectedBehavior.evaluation: Apply diagnostic: 'Will it still work tomorrow? Can someone else take over and run it?'
expectedBehavior.output: Recommend codifying the run into repeatable structure: patterns, documentation, and handoff protocol

diagnosticQuestions: Will it still work tomorrow under different conditions?
Can someone else take over and run it?
Is the success reproducible or was it situational?
Has the successful run been codified into reusable patterns or documentation?
recommendation: Extract structural lessons from the successful run; write patterns to memory/patterns/; create handoff documentation; add Evolution stage to all future runs

passFailCriteria.PASS: Result-Chasing pattern detected; recommendation to codify into repeatable structure; evolution writeback plan created
passFailCriteria.FAIL: Single run accepted as permanent solution; no reproducibility or handoff analysis

input: Our system has one mega-agent that does everything (Stew-All), but also it has no review chain (Governance-Free) and we just copy what worked last time (Result-Chasing)
expectedDiagnosis: Compound Death Patterns: Stew-All + Governance-Free Execution + Result-Chasing Without Structure

expectedBehavior.detection: Identify all three patterns simultaneously; prioritize remediation by severity
expectedBehavior.evaluation: Apply all Four Death Pattern diagnostics; determine interaction effects between patterns
expectedBehavior.output: Prioritized remediation plan: address Stew-All first (split), then Governance-Free (add review chain), then Result-Chasing (codify structure)

diagnosticQuestions: Does the mega-agent have >2 unrelated domains? (Stew-All check)
Who reviews the output? Who reviews the reviewers? (Governance-Free check)
Will the current approach still work tomorrow? (Result-Chasing check)
Are the three patterns reinforcing each other (e.g., no review enables the mega-agent to persist)?
recommendation: Remediate in order: (1) Split Stew-All via Type B pipeline, (2) Add governance chain (review/meta-review/verification/evolution), (3) Codify successful runs into patterns

passFailCriteria.PASS: All three patterns detected and named; interaction effects identified; prioritized remediation plan with specific ordering
passFailCriteria.FAIL: Only one pattern detected; compound effects missed; remediation plan absent or unprioritized

input: We just split a mega-agent into 3 focused agents. Verify they each pass the Five Criteria.
expectedDiagnosis: Post-split verification using Five Criteria for each of the 3 new agents

expectedBehavior.detection: Read each of the 3 new agent definitions
expectedBehavior.evaluation: Apply Five Criteria independently to each agent; verify no new Shattered pattern was introduced by the split
expectedBehavior.output: Evidence table per agent; global check that the split did not create Shattered or Cross-contamination

diagnosticQuestions: Can each new agent be understood, invoked, and produce output independently?
Is each new agent small enough that further splitting would be meaningless?
Do the new agents have clear Own/Do Not Touch boundaries without overlap?
Did the split introduce a Shattered pattern (too fragmented, high coordination cost)?
recommendation: If all 3 agents pass Five Criteria and no Shattered pattern → split is successful; if any fail → targeted remediation per agent

passFailCriteria.PASS: Five Criteria verified per agent with evidence; Shattered and Cross-contamination checks performed post-split; clear verdict per agent
passFailCriteria.FAIL: Verification skipped or performed superficially; new death patterns not checked after splitting
